questions.question: Inside a layout file, which variable holds the rendered output of the page or child layout that is being wrapped?
questions.explanation: Jekyll passes the rendered child output into a layout as the magic variable `content`. Every non-leaf layout must emit `{{ content }}` exactly once or the body disappears.

questions.question: You want a reusable callout box that accepts a `kind` parameter from its caller. How do you read that parameter inside `_includes/callout.html`?
questions.explanation: Parameters passed to an include are scoped under the `include.` namespace. `{{ include.kind }}` reads the value the caller passed via `{% include callout.html kind="warning" %}`.

questions.question: You have a site-wide footer used on every page and a small `<figure>` macro that appears in roughly half your posts. Which composition primitives fit?
choices: Both are layouts - chain them under `default.html`.
Both are includes - call them from each page.
Footer in a layout (it's part of the page skeleton); figure as an include (a reusable inline fragment).
Footer in `_data/`, figure as a layout.
questions.explanation: Layouts express the outside chrome a page is wrapped in; includes express repeatable inside fragments. A site footer is chrome; a figure macro is an inline reusable piece.
